name: Mauritius
avoid: January-March is hotter, wetter, and the most cyclone-prone part of the year, so lagoon cruises and east-coast boat days are the first plans to get reshuffled.
Public buses are very cheap but slower on cross-island transfers and awkward with luggage after dark or on Sundays.
Casela, Chamarel, and Black River Gorges are much easier with a taxi or rental car because the southwest sights are spread out across winding roads.
stopoverNote: If you only have 5-6 days, keep one coastal base in Grand Baie or Flic-en-Flac and do Port Louis, Chamarel, and Île aux Cerfs as day trips instead of changing hotels every night.

cities.name: Port Louis
cities.notes: Mauritius' capital is the heritage-and-market stop for Blue Penny Museum, Aapravasi Ghat, Caudan Waterfront, and the easiest run to SSR Botanical Garden.

cities.name: Grand Baie
cities.notes: The island's best north-coast base for public beaches, boat trips, Pereybere swims, and easy evening dining without a resort bubble.

cities.name: Flic-en-Flac
cities.notes: Mauritius' classic west-coast beach stay with calmer lagoon water, dolphin boat departures from nearby Tamarin, and fast access to Casela and Wolmar.

cities.name: Chamarel
cities.notes: The inland southwest scenic zone for Seven Coloured Earth, waterfall viewpoints, rum tasting, and Black River Gorges lookouts.

cities.name: Île aux Cerfs
cities.notes: Mauritius' signature east-coast lagoon island, usually reached by short speedboat from Trou d'Eau Douce for beach time and day-cruise finishes.

itinerary.sim: my.t or Emtel prepaid SIM/eSIM are the easiest airport pickups; budget roughly ₹900–₹1,800 for enough data to run maps, WhatsApp, and booking confirmations around the island.

Port Louis.name: Port Louis
Port Louis.note: Use Port Louis for museums, market walks, and the easy Pamplemousses garden loop before moving to the beaches.

days.theme: Aapravasi Ghat, Blue Penny Museum and the Caudan Waterfront

activities.name: Taxi from Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam International Airport to Port Louis
activities.tip: Bus 198 is much cheaper at roughly ₹55–₹75 pp but takes longer and is awkward with luggage.

activities.name: Blue Penny Museum admission
activities.tip: The current adult foreigner ticket is MUR 325, so carry a card or small cash for the Caudan Waterfront counter.

activities.name: Aapravasi Ghat
Port Louis Central Market and Chinatown walk
Caudan Waterfront promenade and craft stalls

days.theme: Citadel views, SSR Botanical Garden and a gentler second city day

activities.name: Fort Adelaide (Citadel) viewpoint
Local bus or taxi round-trip to Pamplemousses Botanical Garden
activities.tip: Buses are cheap but slower; a taxi saves time if you want to combine the garden with extra Port Louis stops.

activities.name: Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam Botanical Garden admission
activities.tip: Adult foreigner entry is MUR 300; giant water lilies and talipot palms are the main highlights.

activities.name: Jummah Mosque and old city lane walk
Waterfront sunset stop at Caudan

Grand Baie.name: Grand Baie
Grand Baie.note: Stay here for the north's easiest beach-hopping, nightlife, and boat access to Coin de Mire, Gabriel Island, and Pereybere.

days.theme: North-coast transfer, Pereybere swim and a Grand Baie sunset

activities.name: Port Louis to Grand Baie by local bus or taxi
activities.tip: Bus fares are usually MUR 35–44 per person, while taxis are faster if you have beach luggage.

activities.name: Grand Baie Public Beach and La Cuvette swim stop
Pereybere Beach hop by local bus or taxi
Sunset speedboat cruise around Grand Baie and Coin de Mire

meals: The Beach House Restaurant & Beach Bar

days.theme: North-islet day cruise and an easier evening back in town

activities.name: Catamaran or speedboat day trip to Gabriel Island and Flat Island
activities.tip: Book the day before if winds are calm; north-coast departures usually leave from Grand Baie around 8:30-9:00 a.m.

activities.name: Grand Baie Bazaar and Sunset Boulevard walk
Tamil Surya Oudaya Sangam Temple photo stop
Short taxi hops between beach and dinner venues

Flic-en-Flac.name: Flic-en-Flac
Flic-en-Flac.note: Base here for west-coast beach time plus quick access to Casela, Tamarin Bay dolphin trips, and Wolmar lagoon hotels.

days.theme: Cross-island transfer, Flic-en-Flac beach and west-coast snorkelling

activities.name: Grand Baie to Flic-en-Flac by bus via Port Louis or direct taxi
activities.tip: Two buses keep costs low; a taxi or rental car makes it much easier if you are hotel-hopping.

activities.name: Flic-en-Flac public beach and Casuarina line walk
Tamarin Bay shared dolphin-watch and snorkelling speedboat
Beach lounger or sunset drink budget

days.theme: Casela, Wolmar lagoon and a flexible west-coast evening

activities.name: Casela World of Adventures standard admission
activities.tip: The current standard adult ticket is MUR 1,525; quad biking, zip lines, and safari extras are priced separately.

activities.name: Taxi round-trip between Flic-en-Flac and Casela
Wolmar beach kayak or paddleboard rental
Rental car for Tamarin and Rivière Noire scenic loop

Chamarel.name: Chamarel
Chamarel.note: Chamarel is the inland scenery day: coloured dunes, waterfall viewpoints, rum estate stops, and Black River Gorges lookouts all fit well together.

days.theme: Seven Coloured Earth, Black River Gorges and southwest viewpoints

activities.name: Flic-en-Flac to Chamarel by taxi, bus connection, or rental car
activities.tip: A rental car is the most practical option because the viewpoints and distillery are spread out along winding roads.

activities.name: Chamarel Seven Coloured Earth Geopark admission
activities.tip: The adult non-resident ticket is MUR 750 and includes the waterfall viewpoint and tortoise park.

activities.name: Black River Gorges National Park viewpoints and short trails
activities.tip: There is no park entry fee, but bring water because services are limited once you leave the village.

activities.name: Rhumerie de Chamarel guided tasting or lunch stop
Chamarel waterfall roadside viewpoint stops

Île aux Cerfs.name: Île aux Cerfs
Île aux Cerfs.note: Treat Île aux Cerfs as an east-coast lagoon finish from Trou d'Eau Douce; most travelers sleep on the mainland and use a short speedboat hop to the island.

days.theme: Trou d'Eau Douce launch, lagoon speedboat and an easy beach finale

activities.name: Chamarel to Trou d'Eau Douce by rental car or taxi
activities.tip: It is a long cross-island transfer, so start early if you want a full afternoon on the island.

activities.name: Shared speedboat transfer from Trou d'Eau Douce to Île aux Cerfs
activities.tip: Basic return transfers start around MUR 750; full-day catamaran trips with lunch usually cost much more.

activities.name: Île aux Cerfs beach time and lagoon swim
Full-day catamaran option with Grand River South East waterfall stop
Taxi from Trou d'Eau Douce or Belle Mare to the airport

connections.method: Direct taxi in about 1 hour, or Bus 198 if you want the cheapest public-transport option.
Frequent northbound bus from Port Louis Victoria terminal, direct taxi, or a same-day rental car pickup.
Two-bus route via Port Louis, or a direct taxi/rental car across the island.
Rental car or taxi is easiest for the winding southwest roads; public buses work with a change in Tamarin or Quatre Bornes.
Drive or taxi to Trou d'Eau Douce, then take a short shared speedboat to the island.

extras: May through December is the driest and most comfortable stretch for this route; January to March is hotter, wetter, and the most disruption-prone part of the year.
Mauritius drives on the left, and an economy rental car usually runs about MUR 900-1,500 per day, or roughly ₹1,620-₹2,700 before extra insurance and fuel.
Local buses are cash-based and very cheap, usually MUR 30-44 for longer intercity rides, but they are slower on cross-island routes and lighter on luggage space.
Black River Gorges National Park itself is free; the main paid southwest sight is Chamarel Seven Coloured Earth, while Casela charges separately for most add-on adventure activities.
Île aux Cerfs is easiest as a Trou d'Eau Douce boat day rather than an overnight island stay unless you specifically want Bubble Lodge or a Shangri-La east-coast splurge.

Île aux Cerfs: Trou d Eau Douce Mauritius jetty
